methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Zipline
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G)

data.narrative:
Nuro is a Series G autonomous delivery company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, currently valued at $6.0B post-money with $2.4B raised to date. The comp universe consists of four private peers in the same sector—Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Zipline—with no public comparables available at this stage of development and specialization.

Valuation analysis relies on the Stage-Progression method given data constraints. Applying a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier from Series G to the next anticipated round yields $7.5B. A peer-median approach was not applicable; the narrow filter for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints, and relying on all-sector medians would introduce unwarranted noise given the limited and heterogeneous peer set.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $6.4B (low) to $8.6B (high), with a median of $7.5B. This range reflects modest multiple expansion from the last round, consistent with stage progression in capital-efficient frontier markets. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent comparable transactions at this valuation tier in autonomous delivery and the sector's exposure to regulatory timelines and path-to-profitability risks, which could compress or expand multiples materially.
